free_text: Cross sections for the production of residual nuclides
by low- and medium-energy protons from the target
elements C, N, O, Mg, Al, Si, Ca, Ti, V, Mn, Fe, Co,
Ni, Cu, Sr, Y, Zr, Nb, Ba and Au.
The targets in the stacks were pure element foils
All elemental target sets were separated by sets of
three thin Al-foils 0.035 mm and Cu-foils 0.050mm in
order to minimize cross contamination. In addition,
the inner ones of the three thin Al and Cu foils
between the different target elements were used for
*By authors*.Data tables of this work contain the data
for equal energies. These measurements are independent.
This is first publication of this group where new
Needed correction for interference by reactions of
Correction on self-absorption of gamma-rays in the
These uncertainties are absolute ones and
consider all sources of errors affecting the
Uncertainty in the determination of the number
Overall error due to fluctuation in beam
Upper limit of uncertainty of the
Upper limit for the error of absolute
Upper limit for the error of relative
Upper limit of the uncertainty due to
Error in the dead time and pile up losses
in gamma-spectrometry by varying the distance between
sources and detector, dead time was always kept below
10%, where the automatic dead time correction the
measuring devices can be assumed to be correct.
Beam energy uncertainty takes in account the
finite thickness of the foils as well as the energy
Straggling for last target of experiment at Uppsala
beam energy at this target was 58 +-1.5 MeV. This is
upper limit for experiments with stacks.
Statistical counting error- This source of
uncertainty is included by calculating the errors of
individual photopeaks. The error of background
* Error of monitor cross section was not considered.

New information for STATUS code was added.
to Dr. R.Michel remarks; ref. on INDC(GER)-0052 added.
